frameworks.name: multivoxel pattern analysis (MVPA)
frameworks.one_line: Multivariate decoding from fMRI voxel patterns to extract neural information

frameworks.name: noise correlation theory of population decoding
frameworks.one_line: Extends neuron-scale theory of correlated variability to fMRI voxel populations

frameworks.name: voxel selectivity / mixed selectivity account
frameworks.one_line: Standard view that MVPA succeeds via aggregating weak voxel selectivities

core_question: Do noise correlations between heterogeneous voxel populations contribute to MVPA decoding performance in human fMRI?
key_assumptions: Voxel-level noise correlations are analogous to neuronal noise correlations
Heterogeneous voxel populations carry decodable information beyond individual selectivity
Decoding accuracy is a valid index of population information content
manipulations: Selecting voxels with high vs low noise correlations during classifier training
Comparing GLM selectivity against MVPA classification weights
Simulations of fMRI-like data to test generality
key_findings: Decoding performance enhanced when high noise-correlation voxels are selected for training
Strongly class-selective voxels exhibit high noise correlations with voxels selective for the opposing class
Selectivity and noise correlations exert distinguishable influences on decoding in simulation
Pattern holds as a general property of fMRI data
interpretations: Noise correlations between heterogeneous voxel populations meaningfully contribute to MVPA success
Information underlying MVPA is not reducible to aggregated individual voxel selectivity
